levels.label: White Wolf
levels.description: Build a calm, safe base. Demonstrate fundamental mechanics, awareness, and the ability to train with control.

striking.summary: Demonstrate safe, repeatable striking fundamentals on approved training equipment.
items: Maintain a balanced stance and protective hand position before, during, and after a combination.
Show controlled straight, palm, elbow, and knee mechanics at an instructor-approved intensity.
Use a clear exhale with effort and recover posture after each strike.
Demonstrate partner safety, target discipline, and immediate response to a stop command.

movement.summary: Move without crossing, collapsing posture, or losing visual awareness.
items: Step forward, backward, and laterally while preserving stance and balance.
Pivot and change direction without turning away from the training partner.
Recover a stable base after being lightly redirected or bumped.
Move off a direct line and create a clear path toward an exit.

situationalAwareness.summary: Recognize early warning information and choose a safer option before physical engagement.
items: Identify exits, obstacles, bystanders, and changes in distance during a simple scenario.
Use a clear verbal boundary and non-escalatory posture.
Describe at least two pre-contact cues without assuming intent or escalating unnecessarily.
Choose disengagement, assistance, or movement to safety when those options are available.

breathControl.summary: Use breath and orientation to stay present during foundational drills.
items: Avoid unnecessary breath-holding during movement and pad work.
Use a comfortable exhale during effort without forced breath retention.
Demonstrate a brief post-drill recovery practice using visual orientation and contact with the floor.
Recognize and communicate when intensity should be reduced or paused.

grappling.summary: Establish safe posture and basic positional awareness at low resistance.
items: Demonstrate safe training etiquette, tapping, release, and partner communication.
Use frames and posture to create space without cranking, striking, or forcing joints.
Recognize common grips and maintain balance while seeking an exit.
Complete a basic clinch-position recovery under cooperative resistance.

ground.summary: Protect yourself, orient, and return to standing without rushing.
items: Demonstrate safe falling mechanics on approved mats.
Use a protected ground posture that keeps the head covered and the environment visible.
Create enough space to move without exposing the head or turning away from the situation.
Return to standing through an instructor-approved technical movement while maintaining awareness.

weapons.summary: Prioritize recognition, distance, barriers, escape, and help-seeking.
items: Identify a possible weapon early and immediately adjust distance and positioning.
Use available barriers and exits rather than closing distance unnecessarily.
State the studio priority order: avoid, disengage, escape, communicate, and seek emergency help.
Complete a supervised awareness scenario without attempting an unsanctioned disarm.

levels.label: Yellow Wolf
levels.description: Carry the White Wolf foundation into connected movement. Show reliable basics, early defensive choices, and calm participation under light pressure.

striking.summary: Connect foundational strikes and defenses while preserving stance, breathing, and control.
items: Link two- and three-strike combinations without losing balance or protective structure.
Add a simple defensive response before or after a controlled combination.
Adjust range with footwork rather than reaching, leaning, or crowding the partner.
Finish each exchange by recovering posture, awareness, and a path to safety.

movement.summary: Combine basic steps, pivots, and exits during cooperative partner movement.
items: Move in more than one direction while maintaining a useful stance.
Use a pivot or angle change to leave the direct line of pressure.
Recover balance after light contact without freezing or rushing.
Navigate a simple obstacle while keeping the partner and exit in view.

situationalAwareness.summary: Use early awareness, boundaries, and movement to reduce risk in simple scenarios.
items: Identify distance changes and respond before the situation becomes physical.
Pair a clear verbal boundary with balanced posture and visible hands.
Maintain awareness of exits and nearby people while communicating.
Explain when leaving or seeking help is safer than continuing the interaction.

breathControl.summary: Maintain usable breathing through connected drills and recover with intention.
items: Coordinate exhale with combinations and changes of direction.
Notice breath-holding and restore a comfortable breathing rhythm without stopping unnecessarily.
Use visual orientation and grounded contact during a short recovery interval.
Communicate an appropriate need to pause, slow down, or change intensity.

grappling.summary: Use posture, frames, and movement to create safe space under light resistance.
items: Maintain a stable base while a partner applies a cooperative grip or clinch.
Use an approved frame without pushing through the neck, face, or unsafe joint pressure.
Combine posture and an angle change to improve position.
Release immediately when the drill ends or a partner taps.

ground.summary: Connect protection, movement, and a technical return to standing.
items: Recover a protected posture after an instructor-approved fall or transition.
Use frames and hip movement to create safe working space.
Track the partner and surroundings while preparing to stand.
Return to standing with balance and protective structure under cooperative pressure.

weapons.summary: Apply distance, barrier, escape, and communication priorities in simple supervised scenarios.
items: Recognize a possible weapon cue and move toward greater time and distance.
Place an available barrier between yourself and the possible threat.
Identify an exit and communicate clearly while moving toward safety.
Explain why avoidance and escape remain the preferred response.

levels.label: Orange Wolf
levels.description: Connect skills across range and position. Make deliberate choices while timing, resistance, and environmental demands begin to change.

striking.summary: Connect offense, defense, angle, and disengagement at controlled intensity.
items: Respond to a basic cue with an appropriate defense and controlled counter.
Change angle during a combination without crossing the feet or losing posture.
Move between long and close range on approved equipment with target discipline.
Stop striking when space to disengage becomes available.

movement.summary: Select movement that improves position rather than moving by habit.
items: Use lateral movement, pivots, and direction changes in a non-fixed sequence.
Avoid being guided into a wall, corner, or obstacle during light pressure.
Recover stance after a missed step or unexpected change in direction.
Create and maintain an exit line while another task is introduced.

situationalAwareness.summary: Track communication, distance, environment, and changing behavior together.
items: Reassess exits and bystanders as a scenario changes.
Distinguish discomfort, boundary testing, and immediate danger using observable information.
Use proportionate language without threatening or escalating.
Choose a safe next action and explain the information that supported it.

breathControl.summary: Use pacing and recovery skills as drill complexity increases.
items: Maintain functional breathing during a longer combination or movement round.
Adjust pace before technical control deteriorates.
Recover toward a steady rhythm within an instructor-defined interval.
Remain oriented to the room and partner while using a regulation strategy.

grappling.summary: Connect frames, posture, grip awareness, and exits under measured resistance.
items: Recognize when a grip or position compromises balance.
Combine a frame, posture correction, and angle change to improve position.
Move safely between two approved clinch positions.
Choose disengagement when continued control is unnecessary.

ground.summary: Move from a compromised position toward protection, space, and a safe exit.
items: Maintain head protection while changing ground position.
Use an approved frame and movement sequence when the first path is blocked.
Choose whether to remain grounded or stand based on the scenario.
Disengage and stand without turning away from the partner.

weapons.summary: Make risk-first choices when distance, obstacles, or other people complicate escape.
items: Reposition to prevent an obstacle or bystander from blocking the safest exit.
Use distance and barriers instead of reaching toward a suspected weapon.
Choose escape, compliance, or help-seeking within the rules of a supervised scenario.
State appropriate post-incident priorities after reaching safety.

levels.label: Green Wolf
levels.description: Connect fundamentals into fluid decisions. Maintain control while movement, uncertainty, and resistance increase.

striking.summary: Blend striking, defense, movement, and disengagement at moderate intensity.
items: Link controlled combinations while changing angle and range.
Demonstrate defense-to-counter transitions on pads without sacrificing posture.
Scale power appropriately for the drill and partner.
Finish combinations by regaining awareness and moving toward safety.

movement.summary: Use angles and transitions while pressure and environmental constraints increase.
items: Move around obstacles and confined spaces without losing balance.
Change stance or direction while preserving defensive structure.
Use angle changes to avoid being trapped against a wall or corner.
Recover efficient movement after a short fatigue interval.

situationalAwareness.summary: Apply communication and positioning to more complex scenarios.
items: Use verbal de-escalation while maintaining distance and an exit path.
Position safely when more than one person or obstacle is present.
Distinguish between social conflict, boundary violations, and immediate danger.
Explain the decision to leave, seek help, or intervene without unnecessary escalation.

breathControl.summary: Maintain usable breathing and recover deliberately during moderate stress.
items: Coordinate exhale with sustained combinations and movement.
Recover to a steadier breathing rhythm within an instructor-defined interval after a round.
Notice early signs of overactivation and choose an appropriate pacing adjustment.
Use one low-risk regulation practice without disconnecting from situational awareness.

grappling.summary: Maintain posture and build exits under moderate, controlled resistance.
items: Preserve balance and head position during clinch movement.
Use framing, grip-release principles, and angle changes to create an exit.
Navigate a wall-based position without panic or unsafe force.
Transition between two approved escape options when the first is unavailable.

ground.summary: Recover position and stand safely while a partner provides measured resistance.
items: Protect the head and maintain useful frames during positional pressure.
Move from a compromised position toward a safer orientation.
Create distance and stand without turning away from the partner.
Recognize when remaining grounded is safer than standing immediately.

weapons.summary: Make disciplined choices in supervised weapon-awareness scenarios.
items: Use movement and barriers to increase time and distance.
Choose escape or compliance appropriately within the rules of the scenario.
Communicate clearly with bystanders or emergency services after disengagement.
Explain why closing distance or attempting a disarm may increase risk.

levels.label: Blue Wolf
levels.description: Adapt under pressure. Integrate technique, nervous-system awareness, judgment, and responsibility in changing conditions.

striking.summary: Adapt striking choices to range, movement, fatigue, and a changing objective.
items: Transition between ranges while maintaining technical control.
Use striking only long enough to create a safe opportunity to disengage.
Demonstrate precision and restraint during unpredictable pad sequences.
Coach a foundational striking drill with clear safety boundaries.

movement.summary: Move efficiently through non-patterned pressure and environmental complexity.
items: Maintain balance while navigating obstacles, changing surfaces, or limited space.
Use layered footwork and angle changes against non-scripted pressure.
Protect another person while still preserving an exit route.
Demonstrate efficient movement after a demanding conditioning interval.

situationalAwareness.summary: Lead sound decision-making in layered scenarios.
items: Continuously reassess exits, bystanders, secondary risks, and changing behavior.
Use proportionate communication and intervention choices.
Demonstrate awareness of legal, ethical, and community consequences without giving legal advice.
Complete an after-action reflection identifying what reduced or increased risk.

breathControl.summary: Regulate effectively across higher-intensity rounds without losing awareness.
items: Maintain functional breathing during sustained, non-patterned work.
Select a regulation strategy that matches activation, fatigue, or shutdown cues.
Return to useful orientation and communication after a stressful scenario.
Support a partner with simple co-regulation cues while respecting consent.

grappling.summary: Chain positional solutions under meaningful but controlled resistance.
items: Transition between frames, posture, and movement when the first escape is blocked.
Maintain control without relying on pain compliance or unsafe joint pressure.
Recover from a disadvantaged clinch or wall position while preserving awareness.
Explain the safety rationale behind the selected positional response.

ground.summary: Integrate positional recovery, disengagement, and awareness of secondary threats.
items: Move through multiple ground positions without sacrificing head protection.
Choose between controlling space, disengaging, or standing based on the scenario.
Stand under controlled pressure while monitoring the wider environment.
Demonstrate safe partner care and immediate release when the drill ends.

weapons.summary: Use risk-first judgment in complex, supervised scenarios.
items: Recognize how distance, obstacles, additional people, and escape routes change the response.
Demonstrate disciplined avoidance, barrier use, and movement to safety.
Choose post-incident priorities including distance, communication, medical help, and emergency services.
Explain the limitations of technique and why instructor-supervised practice is required.

levels.label: Brown Wolf
levels.description: Refine adaptable skill into consistent performance. Demonstrate precision, restraint, recovery, and readiness to support safe practice.

striking.summary: Refine striking decisions across changing range, pressure, and fatigue.
items: Maintain accuracy, balance, and protective structure during demanding integrated rounds.
Change tools and targets appropriately as range and position change.
Use only the intensity necessary to meet the supervised drill objective.
Recognize and correct a technical breakdown before it becomes unsafe.

movement.summary: Move with economy while managing environmental pressure and another person's safety.
items: Choose efficient angles instead of adding unnecessary steps.
Maintain balance and awareness while assisting or shielding another person.
Adapt movement to confined space, obstacles, fatigue, or mobility needs.
Explain how a positioning choice improved safety or preserved an exit.

situationalAwareness.summary: Demonstrate mature, proportionate decisions in layered scenarios.
items: Track exits, bystanders, secondary risks, communication, and behavior as conditions change.
Choose intervention, disengagement, or help-seeking without escalating unnecessarily.
Recognize when a technically possible action would create unacceptable risk.
Complete a clear after-action reflection grounded in observable decisions.

breathControl.summary: Sustain awareness and deliberate recovery through high-demand practice.
items: Maintain usable breathing and communication during sustained integrated work.
Adjust pace or strategy before activation or fatigue removes safe choice.
Recover orientation and a functional breathing rhythm after a demanding scenario.
Offer a simple consent-based recovery cue without making medical claims.

grappling.summary: Refine positional problem-solving with control, restraint, and adaptability.
items: Chain approved positional responses when the first option is unavailable.
Maintain control without relying on pain compliance or avoidable force.
Adapt frames, posture, and movement for varied partners and resistance.
Explain when releasing, disengaging, or changing objectives is the safer decision.

ground.summary: Apply reliable ground recovery while accounting for pressure and the wider environment.
items: Recover from multiple compromised positions without abandoning head protection.
Use measured pressure while protecting the training partner.
Choose control, disengagement, or standing based on secondary risks.
Maintain awareness and communication through the end of the drill.

weapons.summary: Demonstrate disciplined judgment in advanced supervised weapon-awareness scenarios.
items: Continuously reassess distance, barriers, exits, bystanders, and changing behavior.
Prioritize avoidance and escape even when a physical option is presented.
Identify when scenario speed, intensity, or behavior should be stopped or reduced.
Explain post-incident priorities including safety, communication, medical help, and emergency services.

levels.label: Black Wolf
levels.description: Demonstrate durable skill, restraint, judgment, and the ability to help others train safely without replacing qualified instruction.

striking.summary: Demonstrate adaptable, precise striking and safe leadership.
items: Maintain technical quality across changing ranges, intensity, and fatigue.
Use the minimum necessary force within supervised scenarios and prioritize disengagement.
Identify and correct common foundational errors without shaming the student.
Lead a controlled striking drill with appropriate pacing and safety checks.

movement.summary: Move with economy and teach movement principles under uncertainty.
items: Demonstrate efficient positioning against changing pressure and environment.
Protect balance and awareness while assisting another person.
Adapt movement for different bodies, mobility needs, and training intensity.
Assess movement quality and provide one clear, useful correction.

situationalAwareness.summary: Model ethical judgment, leadership, and reflective decision-making.
items: Lead a scenario briefing that centers avoidance, communication, proportionality, and escape.
Identify when intervention would create more risk than seeking help.
Track multiple variables without losing sight of the safest available outcome.
Facilitate a non-shaming after-action review grounded in observable choices.

breathControl.summary: Use and teach low-risk regulation skills responsibly.
items: Maintain deliberate breathing and communication during demanding integrated rounds.
Recognize when a member should pause, reduce intensity, or seek qualified support.
Guide a brief, consent-based orientation or recovery practice without making medical claims.
Demonstrate personal recovery habits that support sustainable training.

grappling.summary: Solve positional problems with control and coach safe practice.
items: Adapt framing, posture, and movement against varied partners and resistance.
Maintain control while protecting both people from avoidable injury.
Coach a positional drill with clear objectives, limits, and release conditions.
Explain when disengagement is preferable to continued control.

ground.summary: Integrate ground recovery with judgment, restraint, and environmental awareness.
items: Demonstrate reliable recovery from multiple compromised positions.
Return to standing or disengage based on the broader scenario rather than habit.
Protect a training partner while applying realistic, controlled pressure.
Teach a foundational ground-safety sequence with appropriate supervision.

weapons.summary: Demonstrate mature risk judgment and safe scenario leadership.
items: Lead weapon-awareness drills that prioritize avoidance, distance, barriers, escape, and help-seeking.
Communicate the severe limits and uncertainty of any physical response to a weapon.
Stop or redesign a drill when speed, intensity, or behavior becomes unsafe.
Complete an instructor-led capstone scenario using restraint, judgment, and clear post-incident priorities.

categories.label: Striking
categories.description: Controlled striking mechanics, defense, range management, and responsible application.

categories.label: Movement
categories.description: Balance, footwork, angles, posture recovery, and efficient movement under pressure.

categories.label: Situational Awareness
categories.description: Environmental scanning, boundary setting, de-escalation, and sound decisions before contact.

categories.label: Breath Control
categories.description: Breathing and pacing skills that support steadiness, recovery, and deliberate action.

categories.label: Grappling
categories.description: Posture, framing, balance, grip awareness, and controlled positional problem-solving.

categories.label: Ground
categories.description: Protection, movement, space creation, positional recovery, and a safe return to standing.

categories.label: Weapons
categories.description: Risk recognition, distance, barriers, escape priorities, and supervised scenario judgment.
